svg.title: SOC AI Pipeline Architecture
svg.desc: End-to-end diagram from Kali Linux attacker through Windows 10, Splunk, n8n AI agent, and Slack notification

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me about the Kali Linux brute force attack setup used in this SOC lab')
sendPrompt('Explain MITRE ATT&CK T1110 Brute Force technique')
sendPrompt('How is Universal Forwarder configured on Windows 10 for Splunk in this SOC lab?')
g.text: Splunk Universal Forwarder → forwards Security event logs

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is Windows Event ID 4625 and why is it important for brute force detection?')
sendPrompt('Explain the Splunk correlation rule for brute force detection used in this SOC lab')
g.text: Correlation rule: EventCode=4625 | stats count by src_ip
count ≥ 5 within 1 min → trigger alert → webhook to n8n

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me the full Splunk SPL query for brute force correlation rule')
sendPrompt('Explain the n8n workflow for SOC alert analysis in this lab')
sendPrompt('How is the n8n webhook node configured to receive Splunk alerts?')
sendPrompt('What is the Groq AI agent prompt used in n8n for SOC alert analysis including MITRE, IOC, severity and remediation?')
sendPrompt('How does n8n format the AI analysis output before sending to Slack?')
sendPrompt('What does the Slack SOC alert message look like including MITRE, IOC, severity and remediation?')

svg.text: Click any node to learn more
